title: Staleness / re-map refresh process (incl. post-disaster)
context: OSM data drifts and decays: roads change, buildings are built and demolished, facilities open and close. In post-disaster contexts, the map can become outdated rapidly. open-map-gaps needs a documented staleness / re-map refresh process that defines when an area needs a refresh task, how to trigger it (especially for post-disaster change), and who owns the ongoing liaison. Reverts-for-cause must trigger a root-cause review and a gate/tooling fix. A named steward is required for ongoing maintenance.
objective: Document and commit the staleness / re-map refresh process, including triggers, escalation for post-disaster contexts, revert-for-cause handling, and the named steward role.
acceptanceCriteria: A committed process document defines when an area/pack triggers a refresh task (e.g., staleness threshold, post-disaster event, partner request, revert-for-cause).
Post-disaster escalation is addressed: refresh tasks for active disaster areas flag urgent:true and require expedited gate review.
Revert-for-cause handling: each revert triggers a root-cause log entry and a gate/tooling fix task.
A named steward is recorded as the ongoing liaison for maintenance and partner communication.
Document is reviewed by the Maintainer.
resources: validation-013 QA tool for ongoing re-check
OSMF Organised Editing Guidelines — for re-registering refresh efforts
output: A committed staleness / re-map refresh process document covering triggers, post-disaster escalation, revert-for-cause handling, and a named steward, ready to govern ongoing OSM data maintenance.
